citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
The Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC) did not perform a semiannual review of the program for
humane care and use of animals. The last program review was conducted on March 4, 2025, and was due again in
September 2025. The review is overdue by 4 months at the time of inspection.
The semi-annual review helps to ensure protocol changes, personnel changes, adverse events are addressed in a timely
manner. The review also ensures that proper care and welfare of the animals is being maintained per the facility's
program. Correct by ensuring the IACUC conducts a review of the program for the humane use of animals every 6
months.
Correct by March 31, 2026.

citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
The Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC) did not perform a semiannual review of the animal facilities.
The last facility review was conducted on March 4, 2025, and was due again in September 2025. The review is overdue
by 4 months at the time of inspection.
The review is necessary to ensure IACUC oversight of the facility, ensure repairs or deficiencies are noted and
addressed, and to ensure the welfare of the animals being housed within the facility. Correct by ensuring the IACUC
conducts semiannual reviews of the facility's animal facilities.
Correct by March 31, 2026.

citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care.
citations.narrative:
During the inspection three different anesthesia machine vaporizers in the surgery room were observed overdue for a
routine inspection and calibration. The three machines had calibration stickers stating they were due for calibration in
September 2025. When inquiring with the facility representative if the machines have been calibrated recently it was
provided that the last documented calibration was performed on September 4th, 2024. The facility representative
confirmed this service was not performed in 2025.
Anesthesia machine vaporizers that are not serviced and calibrated regularly could result in the incorrect dosage of
anesthetic agents being delivered to the animal causing complications or harm to the animal while under anesthesia.
Correct by scheduling the anesthesia machines for routine servicing/calibration and ensuring the use of appropriate
methods to prevent, control, diagnose, and treat diseases and injuries.
Correct by February 19th, 2026

The facility's annual report for fiscal year 2025, due to the Deputy Administrator on or before December 1st, 2025 which
was extended to December 31st, 2025, was not submitted prior to the inspection on January 29th, 2026. APHIS officials
inquired with the facility representative if an annual report was submitted by the facility the representative stated he had
issues with submitting the report. When asked if the facility representative reached out to the Field Office for assistance
he stated he did not contact anyone for assistance in submitting the report. The facility representative provided a copy of
the annual report dated January 12th, 2026 signed by the Institutional Official.
Timely submission of the annual report is essential to assure that professionally acceptable standards governing the care
and use of animals were followed during the previous fiscal year. The facility shall submit an annual report to the Deputy
Administrator on or before December 1st of each calendar year.
Correct by sending in the annual report by February 19th, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representatives.
n

narrative:
2.31(c)(1) 
   
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). 
The Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC) did not perform a semiannual review of the program for 
humane care and use of animals. The last program review was conducted on March 4, 2025, and was due again in 
September 2025. The review is overdue by 4 months at the time of inspection.  
 
The semi-annual review helps to ensure protocol changes, personnel changes, adverse events are addressed in a timely 
manner. The review also ensures that proper care and welfare of the animals is being maintained per the facility's 
program. Correct by ensuring the IACUC conducts a review of the program for the humane use of animals every 6 
months.  
 
Correct by March 31, 2026. 
2.31(c)(2) 
   
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). 
The Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC) did not perform a semiannual review of the animal facilities. 
The last facility review was conducted on March 4, 2025, and was due again in September 2025. The review is overdue 
by 4 months at the time of inspection.  
 
The review is necessary to ensure IACUC oversight of the facility, ensure repairs or deficiencies are noted and 
addressed, and to ensure the welfare of the animals being housed within the facility. Correct by ensuring the IACUC 
conducts semiannual reviews of the facility's animal facilities.  
 
Correct by March 31, 2026. 
2.33(b)(2) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care. 
During the inspection three different anesthesia machine vaporizers in the surgery room were observed overdue for a 
routine inspection and calibration. The three machines had calibration stickers stating they were due for calibration in 
September 2025. When inquiring with the facility representative if the machines have been calibrated recently it was

provided that the last documented calibration was performed on September 4th, 2024. The facility representative 
confirmed this service was not performed in 2025.  
 
Anesthesia machine vaporizers that are not serviced and calibrated regularly could result in the incorrect dosage of 
anesthetic agents being delivered to the animal causing complications or harm to the animal while under anesthesia. 
Correct by scheduling the anesthesia machines for routine servicing/calibration and ensuring the use of appropriate 
methods to prevent, control, diagnose, and treat diseases and injuries. 
 
Correct by February 19th, 2026 
2.36(a) 
   
Annual report. 
The facility's annual report for fiscal year 2025, due to the Deputy Administrator on or before December 1st, 2025 which 
was extended to December 31st, 2025, was not submitted prior to the inspection on January 29th, 2026. APHIS officials 
inquired with the facility representative if an annual report was submitted by the facility the representative stated he had 
issues with submitting the report. When asked if the facility representative reached out to the Field Office for assistance 
he stated he did not contact anyone for assistance in submitting the report. The facility representative provided a copy of 
the annual report dated January 12th, 2026 signed by the Institutional Official.  
 
Timely submission of the annual report is essential to assure that professionally acceptable standards governing the care 
and use of animals were followed during the previous fiscal year. The facility shall submit an annual report to the Deputy 
Administrator on or before December 1st of each calendar year.  
 
Correct by sending in the annual report by February 19th, 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representatives.
